merged_events.event_description: Diplomatic relations were established on August 6, 1992, but no physical mission or ambassador was yet in place.

evidence.quote: Diplomatic relations were established on August 6, 1992, when President George H.W. Bush announced the decision during remarks to the press.

merged_events.event_description: A physical American Embassy to Bosnia and Herzegovina was established on November 10, 1993, on the premises of the American Embassy in Vienna, Austria — not yet in the capital Sarajevo.

evidence.quote: a physical American Embassy to Bosnia and Herzegovina was not established until November 10, 1993, on the premises of the American Embassy in Vienna, Austria.

merged_events.event_description: The American Embassy in Sarajevo (the capital) was established on July 4, 1994, with Jackovich as Ambassador.

evidence.quote: The American Embassy in Sarajevo was established on July 4, 1994, with Jackovich as Ambassador.

merged_events.event_description: U.S. Embassy established in Bosnia and Herzegovina when the first Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary, Victor Jackovich, presented credentials.

evidence.quote: Victor Jackovich, Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1992-05-12. Credentials presented: 1993-06-23. Left post on 1995-04-17.

matched.notes: Perfect match. Both CSV and RDCR agree on 1994-07-04 as the date the American Embassy in Sarajevo was established.

missing_from_csv.event_description: Diplomatic relations were established on August 6, 1992, when President George H.W. Bush announced the decision. No physical mission or ambassador was yet in place.
missing_from_csv.notes: RDCR explicitly states diplomatic relations were established on this date. This is a candidate for addition as the initial establishment of relations (status 'None' since no mission or ambassador existed yet).

discrepancies.extracted_value: RDCR (Event 1): 1993-11-10, physical Embassy established in Vienna; POCOM (Event 3): 1993-06-23, Embassy (credential presentation)
discrepancies.reasoning: This CSV row conflates two distinct events. (1) On 1993-06-23, Ambassador Jackovich presented credentials (POCOM Event 3) — this is a personnel event marking the start of an ambassador's nonresident service, since no physical embassy existed yet. The CSV date of 1993-06-23 and status 'Ambassador Nonresident' aligns with this interpretation. (2) On 1993-11-10, RDCR (Event 1) says a physical American Embassy was established on the premises of the Embassy in Vienna — this is the institutional date for the mission opening, though it was located outside Bosnia. Per source priority rules, RDCR's institutional date (1993-11-10) should take precedence over POCOM's credential date (1993-06-23) for the mission establishment event. Suggested split: First event — 1993-06-23, 'Ambassador Nonresident' (ambassador credentialed but no physical mission yet). Second event — 1993-11-10, 'Embassy' (physical embassy established in Vienna, per RDCR). Then the existing CSV Row 2 (1994-07-04) captures the relocation/establishment of the Embassy in Sarajevo proper.
